+{"id":"bd-bkul","title":"Simplify wisp architecture: single DB with ephemeral flag","description":"
## Problem

The current wisp architecture uses a separate .beads-wisp/ directory with its own database. This creates unnecessary complexity:

- Separate directory structure and database initialization
- Parallel routing logic in both beads and gastown
- Merge queries from two stores for inbox/list operations
- All this for what is essentially a boolean flag

## Current State

Gastown mail code has to:
- resolveWispDir() vs resolveBeadsDir()
- Query both, merge results
- Route deletes to correct store

## Proposed Solution

Single database with an ephemeral boolean field on Issue. Behavior:
- bd create --ephemeral sets the flag
- JSONL export SKIPS ephemeral issues (they never sync)
- bd list shows all by default, --ephemeral / --persistent filters
- bd mol squash clears the flag (promotes to permanent, now exports)
- bd wisp gc deletes old ephemeral issues from the db
- No separate directory, no separate routing

## Migration

1. Update beads to support ephemeral flag in main db
2. Update bd wisp commands to work with flag instead of separate dir
3. Update gastown mail to use simple --ephemeral flag (remove all dual-routing)
4. Deprecate .beads-wisp/ directory pattern

## Acceptance Criteria

- Single database for all issues (ephemeral and persistent)
- ephemeral field on Issue type
- JSONL export skips ephemeral issues
- bd create --ephemeral works
- bd mol squash promotes ephemeral to persistent
- Gastown mail uses simple flag, no dual-routing
","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-24T20:06:27.980055-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T20:07:31.209176-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-bqcc","title":"Consolidate maintenance commands into bd doctor --fix","description":"Per rsnodgrass in GH#692:
> "The biggest improvement to beads from an ergonomics perspective would be to prune down commands. We have a lot of 'maintenance' commands that probably should just be folded into 'bd doctor --fix' automatically."

Current maintenance commands that could be consolidated:
- clean - Clean up temporary git merge artifacts
- cleanup - Delete closed issues and prune expired tombstones
- compact - Compact old closed issues
- detect-pollution - Detect and clean test issues
- migrate-* (5 commands) - Various migration utilities
- repair-deps - Fix orphaned dependency references
- validate - Database health checks

Proposal:
1. Make `bd doctor` the single entry point for health checks
2. Add `bd doctor --fix` to auto-fix common issues
3. Deprecate (but keep working) individual commands
4. Add `bd doctor --all` for comprehensive maintenance

This would reduce cognitive load for users - they just need to remember 'bd doctor'.

Note: This is higher impact but also higher risk - needs careful design to avoid breaking existing workflows.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"beads/capable","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:31.466556-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T01:33:25.732363-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T01:33:25.732363-08:00","close_reason":"Merged to main"}
 {"id":"bd-bs5j","title":"Release v0.33.2","description":"Version bump workflow for beads release 0.33.2.

## Variables
- `0.33.2` - The new version number (e.g., 0.31.0)
- `2025-12-21` - Release date (YYYY-MM-DD format)

## Workflow Steps
1. Kill running daemons
2. Run tests and linting
3. Bump version in all files (10 files total)
4. Update cmd/bd/info.go with release notes
5. Commit and push version bump
6. Create and push git tag
7. Update Homebrew formula
8. Upgrade local Homebrew installation
9. Verify installation

## Files Updated by bump-version.sh
- cmd/bd/version.go
- .claude-plugin/plugin.json
- .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
- integrations/beads-mcp/pyproject.toml
- integrations/beads-mcp/src/beads_mcp/__init__.py
- README.md
- npm-package/package.json
- cmd/bd/templates/hooks/* (4 files)
- CHANGELOG.md

## Manual Step Required
- cmd/bd/info.go - Add versionChanges entry with release notes","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:10:13.759062-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:29:31.791368-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-21T17:29:31.791368-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"epic","wisp":true}

 {"id":"bd-f7p1","title":"Add tests for mol spawn --attach","description":"Code review (bd-obep) found no tests for the spawn --attach functionality.

**Test cases needed:**
1. Basic attach - spawn proto with one --attach
2. Multiple attachments - spawn with --attach A --attach B
3. Attach types - verify sequential vs parallel bonding
4. Error case: attaching non-proto (missing template label)
5. Variable aggregation - vars from primary + attachments combined
6. Dry-run output includes attachment info

**Implementation notes:**
- Tests should use in-memory storage
- Create test protos, spawn with attachments, verify dependency structure
- Check that sequential uses 'blocks' type, parallel uses 'parent-child'","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T10:58:16.766461-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:33:12.136215-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:33:12.136215-08:00","close_reason":"Added 6 tests for mol spawn --attach: basic attach, multiple attachments, sequential/parallel bond types, non-proto validation, variable aggregation, and dry-run output","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-f7p1","depends_on_id":"bd-obep","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-21T10:58:16.767616-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-fa2h","title":"🤝 HANDOFF: v0.31.0 released, molecules discussion","description":"Session completed 0.31.0 release and had important molecules discussion.

## Completed
- v0.31.0 released (deferred status, audit trail, directory labels, etc.)
- Fixed lint issues, hook version markers, codesigning
- All CI green, artifacts verified

## Filed Issues
- bd-usro: Rename template instantiate → bd mol bond
- bd-y8bj: Auto-detect identity for bd mail (P1 bug)
- gt-975: Molecule execution support for polecats/crew
- gt-976: Crew lifecycle support in Deacon

## Key Insight
Molecules are the future - TodoWrite is ephemeral, molecules are persistent institutional memory on the world chain. I tried to use TodoWrite for version bump and missed steps (codesigning, MCP verification). Molecules would have caught this.

## Next Steps
- bd mol bond implementation is priority
- Max has gt-976 for crew lifecycle (enables automated refresh mid-molecule)

Check bd ready and gt-975/976 status.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"message","assignee":"beads/crew/dave","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:23:09.889562-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:52:18.467069-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:52:18.467069-08:00","close_reason":"Stale handoff message - work completed","sender":"Steve Yegge","wisp":true}

+{"id":"bd-fej5","title":"bd hook: detect agent from cwd instead of defaulting to $USER","description":"**Problem:**
`bd hook` without `--agent` defaults to `$USER` (e.g., "stevey") instead of detecting the agent identity from the current working directory.

**Expected behavior:**
When running from `/Users/stevey/gt/beads/crew/dave`, the agent should be detected as `beads/crew/dave`.

**Current behavior:**
```bash
$ pwd
/Users/stevey/gt/beads/crew/dave
$ bd hook
Hook: stevey
  (empty)

$ bd hook --agent beads/crew/dave
Hook: beads/crew/dave
  📌 bd-hobo (mol) - open
```

**Fix:**
bd hook should use the same cwd-based agent detection that other commands use (similar to how `gt mail` determines identity from cwd).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-24T20:01:27.613892-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T20:01:27.613892-08:00"}

Merge them, deprecate bd template commands.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T23:52:13.208972-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T00:01:59.283765-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T00:01:59.283765-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented mol commands with deprecation for template commands"}

 {"id":"bd-fi05","title":"bd sync fails with orphaned issues and duplicate ID conflict","description":"After fixing the deleted_at TEXT column scanning bug (commit 18b1eb2), bd sync still fails with two issues:

1. Orphan Detection Warning: 12 orphaned child issues whose parents no longer exist (bd-cb64c226.* and bd-cbed9619.*)

2. Import Failure: UNIQUE constraint failed for bd-360 - this tombstone exists in both DB and JSONL

Error: "Import failed: error creating depth-0 issues: bulk insert issues: failed to insert issue bd-360: sqlite3: constraint failed: UNIQUE constraint failed: issues.id"

Fix options:
- Delete orphaned child issues with bd delete
- Resolve bd-360 duplicate (in deletions.jsonl vs tombstone in DB)
- Reset sync branch: git branch -f beads-sync main && git push --force-with-lease origin beads-sync","notes":"Fixed tombstone constraint violation bug. When deleting closed issues, the CHECK constraint (status = 'closed') = (closed_at IS NOT NULL) was violated because CreateTombstone didn't clear closed_at. Fix: set closed_at = NULL in tombstone creation SQL.

The sync data corruption (orphaned issues in beads-sync branch) requires manual cleanup: reset sync branch with 'git branch -f beads-sync main && git push --force-with-lease origin beads-sync'","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-13T07:14:33.831346-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T10:50:48.545465-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T07:30:33.843986-08:00"}
